text: Choose the word that best completes the sentence: The politician's _____ remarks alienated even his most loyal supporters.
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: ELATED is to DESPONDENT as TRANSPARENT is to:
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: Which word is most nearly opposite in meaning to EPHEMERAL?
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: Read the statement: 'All managers attended the meeting. Some managers are engineers.' Which conclusion is valid?
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: Choose the pair that best expresses a relationship similar to ARCHITECT : BLUEPRINT
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: Select the word that does NOT belong with the others:
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: The report was so _____ that even experts found it difficult to understand the key findings.
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: PROLIFIC is to SCARCE as ELABORATE is to:
subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: If 'no politicians are honest' and 'all honest people are trustworthy,' which must be true?

options.text: None of the above can be determined

subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: Which sentence uses the word 'discrete' correctly?

options.text: She was very discrete about the surprise party
The data was divided into discrete categories
He made a discrete attempt to leave early
Please be discrete with this information

subject: Verbal Reasoning

text: A company's revenue increased from ₦2.4 million to ₦3.0 million. What is the percentage increase?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: If 3 workers can complete a project in 12 days, how many days would 4 workers need to complete the same project?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: What is the next number in the series: 2, 6, 18, 54, ...?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: A product costs ₦5,000 after a 20% discount. What was the original price?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:5. If there are 40 students, how many boys are there?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: An investment of ₦100,000 earns 8% simple interest per year. What is the total value after 3 years?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: A train travels 240 km in 3 hours. If it increases speed by 20 km/h, how long will it take to travel 360 km?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: In a survey, 60% of 500 respondents preferred Product A. If the margin of error is ±4%, what is the minimum number who preferred Product A?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: What is the next number in the series: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ...?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: A company's expenses are distributed as: Salaries 45%, Rent 20%, Utilities 15%, Supplies 10%, Other 10%. If total expenses are ₦2,000,000, how much is spent on Rent and Utilities combined?
subject: Numerical Reasoning

text: Which shape completes the pattern? In each row, a circle gains one additional line segment. Row 3 has shapes with 0, 1, and ?.
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: In a sequence, each figure rotates 45° clockwise and changes from filled to hollow alternately. What is the 5th figure if the 1st is a filled square at 0°?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: Find the odd one out: All shapes are symmetrical along at least one axis except one.
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: A pattern shows: 1 dot → 3 dots → 6 dots → 10 dots. How many dots come next?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: In a grid, shapes follow a rule: each row contains exactly one circle, one triangle, and one square. Each column has exactly one filled, one striped, and one hollow shape. Which fills the empty cell in row 3, column 2?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: If A → B means 'A is smaller than B', and the sequence shows: small circle → medium circle → ? → large square. What fills the gap?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: A cube is painted red on all sides, then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many small cubes have exactly two painted faces?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: Looking at a mirror image: if the original shows an arrow pointing to the upper-right, the mirror image shows the arrow pointing to the:
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: A sequence alternates between adding sides and rotating. Starting with a triangle facing up: Triangle up → Square right → Pentagon up → ? What comes next?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: If you fold a piece of paper twice and punch a hole through all layers, how many holes appear when unfolded?
subject: Abstract Reasoning

text: You notice a colleague consistently taking credit for work done by the team. What is the most appropriate action?

options.text: Publicly confront them in the next team meeting
Start taking credit for their work in return
Speak to them privately about giving proper credit to the team
Report them immediately to senior management

subject: Situational Judgement

text: A client sends an email with an urgent request outside your area of expertise. Your supervisor is unavailable. What should you do?

options.text: Attempt to handle it yourself to show initiative
Ignore the email until your supervisor returns
Acknowledge receipt, explain you'll connect them with the right person, and find a qualified colleague
Forward the email to all departments and hope someone handles it

subject: Situational Judgement

text: You discover a minor error in a report that has already been submitted to a client. The error is unlikely to affect the overall conclusions. What do you do?

options.text: Ignore it since it doesn't affect the conclusions
Inform your supervisor and send a correction to the client
Wait to see if the client notices the error
Blame the error on a colleague who helped with the report

subject: Situational Judgement

text: Two team members are in a heated disagreement about the approach to a project. As the team lead, what is the best course of action?

options.text: Side with the more senior team member
Let them work it out themselves
Listen to both perspectives, facilitate discussion, and guide toward a solution aligned with project goals
Assign the project to someone else entirely

subject: Situational Judgement

text: You are asked to work late to meet a deadline, but you have a personal commitment. What is the most professional response?

options.text: Refuse outright and leave on time
Cancel your personal commitment without question
Explain your situation, offer to come in early the next day or find an alternative solution to meet the deadline
Agree to stay but do minimal work out of resentment

subject: Situational Judgement

text: You overhear a colleague sharing confidential company information with an external party. What should you do?

options.text: Confront the colleague loudly in front of others
Report the incident to your supervisor or compliance officer
Share the story with other colleagues to get their opinions

subject: Situational Judgement

text: A new employee is struggling to learn the systems and processes. Your workload is already heavy. What is the best approach?

options.text: Tell them to figure it out on their own like you did
Offer to help during a break or set up a brief daily check-in to guide them
Report them to HR for poor performance
Do their work for them to avoid delays

subject: Situational Judgement

text: Your manager asks you to implement a change that you believe will negatively impact the project. What do you do?

options.text: Implement it without question since they are your manager
Refuse to do it and explain why it's wrong
Respectfully present your concerns with data or evidence, then follow the final decision
Implement it poorly so it fails and proves your point

subject: Situational Judgement

text: During a team presentation, you realize you have made an error in your slides. What is the best action?

options.text: Continue presenting and hope no one notices
Stop the presentation and spend time fixing the slides
Acknowledge the error briefly, correct it verbally, and continue professionally
Blame the error on technical difficulties

subject: Situational Judgement

text: You receive feedback from a client that your team's service has been declining. You believe the feedback is unfair. How do you respond?

options.text: Argue with the client and defend your team's performance
Thank them for the feedback, investigate the concerns, and develop an action plan for improvement
Ignore the feedback since you believe it's unfair
Forward the complaint to your team members so they feel pressured to improve

subject: Situational Judgement
